external.description:
Amazon SageMaker Unified Studio notebooks now support import/export capabilities, enabling migration from JupyterLab and other notebook platforms. This release also introduces developer acceleration features including cell reordering, keyboard shortcuts, cell renaming, and multi-line SQL support, designed to enhance productivity for data engineers and data scientists professionals working with notebook-based workflows.

 The new import/export functionality supports .ipynb, .json, and .py formats while preserving cell types, outputs, execution history, and metadata, making platform migration straightforward.. You can export notebooks in four formats including Jupyter notebook with requirements (.zip), standard .ipynb, Python scripts (.py), and SageMaker Unified Studio native format (.json). Developer acceleration features enable you to reorder cells without copy-paste duplication, assign custom names to cells for improved navigation in large notebooks, use familiar keyboard shortcuts for faster development, and execute multiple SQL statements in a single cell with results displayed in separate tabs for easy comparison and analysis.

 This feature is available in https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker-unified-studio/latest/adminguide/supported-regions.html. To learn more, visit the https://aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/unified-studio/notebooks/ and https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker-unified-studio/latest/userguide/export-share-notebooks.html.
external.title: Amazon SageMaker Unified Studio adds notebook import/export and developer acceleration features

Amazon Kinesis Video Streams (KVS) now supports WebRTC (Real-Time Communication) in AWS GovCloud (US) Regions, bringing real-time, low-latency media and data stre...

#AWS #AmazonKinesisVideoStreams #AwsGovcloudUs

external.description:
Amazon Kinesis Video Streams (KVS) now supports WebRTC (Real-Time Communication) in AWS GovCloud (US) Regions, bringing real-time, low-latency media and data streaming to security-sensitive workloads in the cloud. Amazon Kinesis Video Streams makes it easy to securely ingest, store, and process video from connected devices at scale. WebRTC support extends this capability to real-time, two-way media streaming, enabling sub-second latency for mission-critical applications.

 With this update, AWS customers and AWS Partners who operate in the AWS GovCloud (US) Regions can now build and deploy real-time video streaming applications without compromising on compliance. For example, the update enables developers within these agencies to support critical use cases including live surveillance feeds for border security and military installations, real-time body camera streaming for law enforcement with data residency compliance, remote drone and UAV video monitoring for defense operations, and Internet of Things (IoT) smart infrastructure monitoring for state and local governments, all while meeting Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program (FedRAMP) High and other regulatory requirements.

 This feature is available today in AWS GovCloud (US-East) and AWS GovCloud (US-West). To get started, visit the https://aws.amazon.com/kinesis/video-streams/ and see the https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kinesisvideostreams-webrtc-dg/latest/devguide/availability.html for complete regional availability.

Amazon Quick Enables Sparklines for Inline Trend Visualization in Tables

https://aws.amazon.com/quick/quicksight/ now supports sparklines, enabling authors to embed compact, inline trend visualizations directly within table cells. Sparklines allow users to display line ...

#AWS #AmazonQuicksight

external.description:
https://aws.amazon.com/quick/quicksight/ now supports sparklines, enabling authors to embed compact, inline trend visualizations directly within table cells. Sparklines allow users to display line or area charts inside individual table columns, providing at-a-glance trend context alongside their data without requiring separate chart visuals. Authors can add sparklines to tables by configuring a metric with a date dimension, and Quick automatically renders a miniature trend line within each row.

 This enhancement helps organizations quickly identify patterns, anomalies, and directional trends within tabular data — such as revenue over time by product, weekly ticket volumes by team, or monthly utilization by region — without navigating away from the table view. Sparklines support customization options including visual type (line or area), line color, line interpolation (linear, smooth, or stepped), and Y-axis behavior (shared or independent scaling across rows), giving authors flexibility to tailor the visualization to their dashboard's needs.

 This feature is now available in all Amazon Quick https://docs.aws.amazon.com/quicksuite/latest/userguide/regions.html Learn more about how to https://docs.aws.amazon.com/quick/latest/userguide/format-sparklines.html in Amazon Quick.
external.title: Amazon Quick Enables Sparklines for Inline Trend Visualization in Tables

images.alt:
A softly rendered, storybook-style illustration shows a small white rabbit sitting on a nest made of twigs and grass. The rabbit faces slightly right, holding a single reddish egg close to its chest with both front paws. Its fur is delicately textured with fine ink-like lines and watercolor shading, giving it a soft, tactile presence. One ear is upright while the other tilts slightly back, revealing a warm pink inner ear. Its eye is glossy and reflective, conveying a quiet sense of curiosity and calm.

Around the rabbit, several pastel-colored eggs are scattered, including pale blue and soft peach tones. Behind it, a loosely sketched basket holds more eggs, partially obscured by the rabbit’s body. The ground beneath is lightly shadowed, blending into a dreamy, undefined surface.

Above, the background transitions into a deep, cosmic night sky filled with soft nebula-like textures and scattered stars. Several glowing star shapes hang from thin, vertical strings, suspended like ornaments or gentle lanterns. These stars emit a warm, golden light that softly illuminates the rabbit’s face and the egg it holds, creating a quiet focal point.

The edges of the image are framed with a rough, painterly border, as if the scene was brushed onto textured paper. The overall mood is peaceful and magical, blending elements of Easter imagery with a serene, celestial dreamscape.

Keywords:
rabbit, easter eggs, hanging stars, night sky, watercolor illustration, whimsical animal, soft lighting, storybook scene, celestial background, pastel colors

images.alt:
A wide, cinematic view of a modern high-rise interior at dusk. Floor-to-ceiling windows stretch across the background, revealing a dim, bluish city skyline fading into night. Centered in the room is a low, rectangular leather couch in a rich burnt-orange tone, its smooth surface catching warm highlights. Reclining across it is a bald, middle-aged man with a long, thick, ginger beard. He wears a dark, tailored suit with a slightly relaxed fit, black shoes, and dark sunglasses that obscure his eyes. His posture is casual, one leg crossed over the other, one arm draped along the backrest, the other resting near his thigh. A faint curl of smoke rises near his face, suggesting a recently exhaled breath.

In stark contrast to his composed demeanor, the polished floor in front of the couch is marked by a large, swirling stain in a similar orange hue, as if liquid or pigment has spread outward in organic tendrils. The shape resembles a loose, circular sigil or abstract creature, with branching lines and feathered edges that look almost alive. The stain appears to have seeped into the floor’s glossy surface, creating a ghostly reflection that distorts its form. The lighting splits the scene into two tones, warm highlights around the couch and figure, and cool, shadowed blues beyond the glass, emphasizing the tension between control and something quietly unraveling beneath it.

Keywords:
surreal portrait, modern interior, leather couch, city skyline, bald man, long beard, sunglasses, abstract stain, cinematic lighting, high-rise, occult symbol
